title: seseli montanum subsp. granatense
intro: Seseli montanum subsp. granatense is a sub-species of the Seseli montanum plant, commonly known as Granatense wild carrot or Granatense selder. This plant is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, with medicinal properties attributed to its roots, leaves, and seeds. The roots and seeds of Seseli montanum subsp. granatense have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Seseli montanum subsp. granatense is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal benefits.
